subject: 🏃 Friday — Modified Lower B + Upper Push
html:
Today: Lower B + Upper Push, but we're modifying Lower B. Your knee logged Yellow again yesterday — that's two Yellow running sessions in a row (April 12 and April 16), and the medial-side tendon involvement you described is worth respecting. The Lower A session on Monday also triggered Yellow by afternoon. So today we go cautious on lower body.

Modified Lower B: Do the full warm-up (monster walks, glute bridges, walking lunges). For the main circuit, keep side-lying hip abductions, lateral band walks, and single-leg glute bridges — these are low knee stress and exactly the hip/glute strengthening that protects the knee. Skip Bulgarian split squats and eccentric step-ups today — both load the knee through deep flexion, and until the PT takes a look, we don't want to provoke whatever's going on medially. Do dead bugs and Pallof presses as normal. Skip the wall sit. Do Upper Push in full afterward.

Focus cue: During every lower body exercise, monitor the medial knee. If you feel the tendon awareness ramp up at all, stop that exercise and move on. Zero tolerance today.

Context: Week 1, Day 5 of 15. Base phase. You're 100 days out from race day. The fact that your knee symptoms are medial-side with tendon involvement — not the classic anterior patellofemoral pain — is actually useful information. Good call booking the PT. That diagnosis will let us train smarter, not just more carefully. Sunday's long 8 is still on the board, but Saturday's easy 5 is the next real knee test. We'll decide on the long run after that.
